title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They are reporting that the prisoner has been recaptured.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive reporting structures with perfect infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'm sorry I broke your favorite mug,’ said Kevin to his sister.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with dependent prepositions and gerunds.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't know it was your birthday, so I didn't buy you a present.
questions.afterGap: your birthday, I would have bought you a present.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for hypothetical past events.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure that the keys fell out of your pocket.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for certain past deduction.

questions.firstSentence: The team had only just started the match when it began to hail.
questions.afterGap: the match when it began to hail.

questions.firstSentence: It's a waste of time to try to get a tickets for that show.
questions.afterGap: to get tickets for that show.
